headline: Eaton to power next-gen AI-ready data centres in the tropics through expanded collaboration with NUS-led Sustainable Tropical Data Centre Testbed
summary: Eaton, an intelligent power management company, today announced an expanded collaboration with the Sustainable Tropical Data Centre Testbed (STDCT) – a programme led by the National University of Singapore (NUS), committing about S$3 million across STDCT Phase 1 and Phase 2 to accelerate the validation of AI-driven, high-density data centre power architectures optimized for tropical environments.

content.title: Eaton to power next-gen AI-ready data centres in the tropics through expanded collaboration with NUS-led Sustainable Tropical Data Centre Testbed

thumbnail.caption: Left: Jimmy Yam, Vice-President and General Manager, Electrical Sector, East Asia, Eaton Right: Professor Lee Poh Seng, Programme Director of STDCT and Head of the Department of Mechanical Engineering at the College of Design and Engineering at NUS
